perturbation.original_text: Company agrees that all information, documents, data, materials and the AOC proprietary software systems disclos ed to, or accessed by, Company in connection with, or relat ed to, the RFP or the RFP process, in any form whether oral or written, or in any m edium, including but not limited to any of the following is “Confidential Information ” under the terms of this Agreement: a. The subject matter described in , and referred to, in the RFP, its associated Statements of Work (“SOWs ”) or any other attachments, or during the RFP process (collect ively “AOC Requirements”); b. The RFP document (including all attachments), its content, and all supporting data, materials and a ll other information provided to Company in any form or medium in or in relation to the AOC’s RFP; c. Any information, data, document or material concerning or related to the AOC Requirements, includi ng but not limited to the AOC Requirements described in the RFP, SOWs, and all subsequent information, data, document or ma terials that may be provided to Company from time to time during the RFP process, including but not limited to any information, data, docum ent or materials provided during or in response to any questions from Company or another recipient of the RFP; Attachment 8 Non-Disclosure Agreement for Request for Proposal – California Courts Protective Order Registry Development Page 2 of 5 d. The SOW documents (including all attachments), their content, and all supporting data, materials and a ll other information provided to Company in any form or medium in relation to the AOC’s RFP process; e. All fees including base charges for services, ARC and RRC rates, termination fees, resource baseline, volume discounts, new service rates, and skill categories for services; f. Any information, data, document or material provided to Company in any form or medium to conduct any capability test pilot or demonstration, whether at the Com pany’s facilities or the AOC’s facilities; g. Description of the AOC’s finance, information technology, engineering and purchasing activities; h. The AOC’s objectives and strategies , including without limitation, the AOC’s direction and the AO C’s sourcing objectives; i. The AOC’s standards, policies, procedures and methodologies; j. The AOC’s volume information provided in the RFP; k. Any and all other information, data or materials that Company receives access to or obtains in connection wit h onsite visits, meetings or in any other forms of communication w hether oral or written with AOC personnel and contractors; and l. Any and all other information, data or materials learned by Company through inspection of the AOC’s property regarding the AOC’s products, designs, business plans, st rategies or processes, business opportunities, procurement or supply business pl ans, strategies or process, sales or marketing plans, strategies or processes, technical plans, strategies, or architecture, financial plans, strategies or processes, research, developmen t, know-how, personnel, or third- party confidential information disclosed to Company by the AOC.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees that all information, documents, data, materials and the AOC proprietary software systems disclos ed to, or accessed by, Company in connection with, or relat ed to, the RFP or the RFP process, in any form whether oral or written, or in any m edium, including but not limited to any of the following *may be* considered “Confidential Information ” under the terms of this Agreement: a. The subject matter described in , and referred to, in the RFP, its associated Statements of Work (“SOWs ”) or any other attachments, or during the RFP process (collect ively “AOC Requirements”); b. The RFP document (including all attachments), its content, and all supporting data, materials and a ll other information provided to Company in any form or medium in or in relation to the AOC’s RFP; c. The SOW documents (including all attachments), their content, and all supporting data, materials and a ll other information provided to Company in any form or medium in relation to the AOC’s RFP process; e. All fees including base charges for services, ARC and RRC rates, termination fees, resource baseline, volume discounts, new service rates, and skill categories for services; f. Any information, data, document or material provided to Company in any form or medium to conduct any capability test pilot or demonstration, whether at the Com pany’s facilities or the AOC’s facilities; g. Description of the AOC’s finance, information technology, engineering and purchasing activities; h. The AOC’s objectives and strategies , including without limitation, the AOC’s direction and the AO C’s sourcing objectives; i. The AOC’s standards, policies, procedures and methodologies; j. The AOC’s volume information provided in the RFP; k. Any and all other information, data or materials that Company receives access to or obtains in connection wit h onsite visits, meetings or in any other forms of communication w hether oral or written with AOC personnel and contractors; and l. Any and all other information, data or materials learned by Company through inspection of the AOC’s property regarding the AOC’s products, designs, business plans, st rategies or processes, business opportunities, procurement or supply business pl ans, strategies or process, sales or marketing plans, strategies or processes, technical plans, strategies, or architecture, financial plans, strategies or processes, research, developmen t, know-how, personnel, or third- party confidential information disclosed to Company by the AOC.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is' to '*may be*' weakens the definition of 'Confidential Information', making it ambiguous whether all the listed items are actually considered confidential. This creates an in-text contradiction because section 2 states that 'All Confidential Information disclosed by the AOC to Company shall be received in confidence by Company', creating uncertainty about which information the company is obligated to protect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclos ed by the AOC to Company shall be received in confidence by Company. Co mpany agrees that it shall undertake all necessary and appropriate steps to ensure that the secrecy of the Confidential Information in its possessi on shall be maintained. Company also agrees that it shall treat the Confi dential Information with not less than the same degree of care and confidentiality with which it treats its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care. Company agrees that access to the C onfidential Information shall be given by it only to Attachment 8 Non-Disclosure Agreement for Request for Proposal – California Courts Protective Order Registry Development Page 3 of 5 those of its employees who have a need to know to engage in the business relationship contemplated by this Agr eement and who have signed a non-use and non-disclosure agreement in content at least as protective of the Confidential Information as the provisions hereof, prior to any disclosure of the Confidential Information to such employees.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the listed items 'is' considered confidential information, which imposes a clear obligation. The changed text weakens this by stating the information '*may be*' considered confidential, introducing ambiguity. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that 'All Confidential Information disclosed by the AOC to Company shall be received in confidence by Company.' The 'may be' undermines the confidentially required. This creates a strong contradiction as it is unclear which information the company is obligated to keep confidential.

perturbation.original_text: Company also agrees that it shall treat the Confi dential Information with not less than the same degree of care and confidentiality with which it treats its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Company also agrees that it shall treat the Confi dential Information with *reasonable* care.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'not less than the same degree of care and confidentiality with which it treats its own confidential information, but in no event less than' from Section 2 changes the standard of care expected of the Company with respect to Confidential Information. This alteration creates an in-text contradiction with Section 8, which provides no warranties regarding the Confidential Information's accuracy or completeness, implying that the Company is not held responsible for errors in the information itself, but with this new definition in section 2, it is now unclear on how to approach the liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE AOC MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING ITS ACCURACY, CO MPLETENESS, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2 established a higher standard of care for Confidential Information, requiring the Company to treat it with at least the same degree of care as its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care. The changed text lowers this standard to merely 'reasonable care'. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 8, which disclaims all warranties regarding the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information and now, because of the reasonable care change, creates an ambiguity on how to approach liability as mentioned in the Perturbation Explanation. The removal of the original higher standard in Section 2 introduces ambiguity and inconsistency regarding the company's responsibility, conflicting with the 'AS IS' nature in Section 8.

perturbation.original_text: Company shall not (i) use any portion of the Confidential Information for any purpose except to evaluate the RFP, in cluding assessment of each Statement of Work under the RFP, in order to pr epare its response to the RFP or (ii) disclose the Confidential Information or any part of it to any third party without the prior written consent of the AOC; provi ded, however, that Confidential Information shall not include any informa tion of the AOC that: (a) is already properly known to Company at the time of its disclosure as shown by the Company’s files and records immediately pr ior to the time of disclosure by the AOC to Company; (b) is publicly know n through no action or inaction of Company; (c) is received from a third par ty free to disclose it to Company; (d) is independently developed by the Comp any without use of or reference to the Confidential Information, as s hown by documents and other competent evidence in the Company’s possession; or (e) is communicated to a third party with express written consent of t he AOC.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall not (i) use any portion of the Confidential Information for any purpose except to evaluate the RFP, in cluding assessment of each Statement of Work under the RFP, in order to pr epare its response to the RFP or (ii) disclose the Confidential Information or any part of it to any third party without the prior written consent of the AOC; provi ded, however, that Confidential Information *may* not include any informa tion of the AOC that: (a) is already properly known to Company at the time of its disclosure as shown by the Company’s files and records immediately pr ior to the time of disclosure by the AOC to Company; (b) is publicly know n through no action or inaction of Company; (c) is received from a third par ty free to disclose it to Company; (d) is independently developed by the Comp any without use of or reference to the Confidential Information, as s hown by documents and other competent evidence in the Company’s possession; or (e) is communicated to a third party with express written consent of t he AOC.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not include' to 'may not include' in Section 3 introduces ambiguity. It's now unclear whether the listed exceptions are definite exclusions from the definition of Confidential Information. This contradicts the intent of Section 1, which defines 'Confidential Information' and this section provides carve-outs to this definition. By weakening the carve-outs, it creates uncertainty in how Section 1 is to be interpreted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company agrees that all information, documents, data, materials and the AOC proprietary software systems disclos ed to, or accessed by, Company in connection with, or relat ed to, the RFP or the RFP process, in any form whether oral or written, or in any m edium, including but not limited to any of the following *may be* considered “Confidential Information ” under the terms of this Agreement: a. perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall not include' to 'may not include' introduces ambiguity regarding the exceptions to Confidential Information. The original text clearly defines what is *not* considered Confidential Information. The changed text weakens this definition, creating uncertainty. This ambiguity directly contradicts the contradicted text, which lists specific items that *may be* considered Confidential Information, as this section relies on a clear definition of what constitutes Confidential Information in the first place. By weakening the definition, it makes the contradicted section more vague, implying uncertainty about whether certain types of information will or won't be treated as confidential.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise pr ovided in any future agreem ent, at any time, Company agrees that it shall return to the AOC upon request all documents, records, notebooks, computer media or other stored informati on of any form or type whatsoever containing any Confidentia l Information, including all copies thereof, then in its possession or contro l (directly or indirectly), whether prepared by it or others and it shall at such time immediately discontinue all Attachment 8 Non-Disclosure Agreement for Request for Proposal – California Courts Protective Order Registry Development Page 4 of 5 use of the Confiden tial Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise pr ovided in any future agreem ent, at any time, Company agrees that it *may* return to the AOC upon request all documents, records, notebooks, computer media or other stored informati on of any form or type whatsoever containing any Confidentia l Information, including all copies thereof, then in its possession or contro l (directly or indirectly), whether prepared by it or others and it shall at such time immediately discontinue all Attachment 8 Non-Disclosure Agreement for Request for Proposal – California Courts Protective Order Registry Development Page 4 of 5 use of the Confiden tial Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' introduces optionality for the Company to return Confidential Information. This introduces ambiguity and an in-text contradiction in Section 5 and creates a direct conflict with Section 14, which outlines the survival of obligations after termination. If the company *may* not return the information, it undermines the intended survival clause regarding confidential information disclosed prior to termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall survive until such time as all Confid ential Information disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of Company. Except as otherwise provided in any future agreement, either party may terminate this Agreement by written notice to the other. Not withstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Company 'shall' return confidential information, creating a clear obligation. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may', making the return of confidential information optional. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that all rights and obligations survive termination with respect to confidential information disclosed prior to such termination. If returning confidential information is optional, it undermines the survival clause related to that information. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the obligation to return the confidential information is no longer guaranteed, conflicting with the expectation in the survival clause that obligations related to confidential information continue after termination.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge and agree t hat the Confidential Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC her eunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extr aordinary character, which gives them a peculiar value, the loss of which may not be a dequately or reasonably compensated for in damages in an action at law, and further agree that the breach by Company of any of the pr ovisions of this Agreem ent shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge and agree t hat the Confidential Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC her eunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extr aordinary character, *but the parties make no representation as to the value of such information, and the parties agree that* the breach by Company of any of the pr ovisions of this Agreem ent shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'but the parties make no representation as to the value of such information, and the parties agree that' in Section 9 creates a contradiction. The original text asserts the information has 'a peculiar value' but the addition implies no value is guaranteed or represented. This contrasts with the later statement in the same sentence stating a breach would cause irreparable injury and damage. This contradicts itself and thus creates ambiguity on how to handle the breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge and agree t hat the Confidential Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC her eunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extr aordinary character, *but the parties make no representation as to the value of such information, and the parties agree that* the breach by Company of any of the pr ovisions of this Agreem ent shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage. In such event, the party alleging breach of this Agreement shall be entitled, as a matter of right, without further notice, to require of the other party specific performance of all of the acts and undertakings required of the other party hereunder and to obtain injunctive and other equitable relief in any competent court to prevent the violation or threatened violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party. Neither this provision nor any exer cise by either party of its rights to equitable relief or specific performance herein granted shall constitute a waiver by either party of any other rights which it may have to, damages or otherwise. If either party brings su it to enforce the terms hereof, the successful party in such suit shall be ent itled to receive all of its reasonable costs of litigation, including attorneys' fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'but the parties make no representation as to the value of such information' to a sentence that originally claimed the confidential information had a 'peculiar value'. This addition directly contradicts the original assertion of value, and therefore also undermines the subsequent claim that a breach would cause 'irreparable injury and damage'. This creates a strong contradiction within the contradicted text, as the parties simultaneously acknowledge value and disclaim any representation of value, which directly affects the enforceability of the clause regarding breach and remedies.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject hereof and any repr esentation, promise or condition not contained herein, or any amendment hereto or waiver hereunder shall not be binding on either party unless in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the party to be bound thereby.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains *what is believed to be* the sole and entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject hereof and any repr esentation, promise or condition not contained herein, or any amendment hereto or waiver hereunder shall not be binding on either party unless in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the party to be bound thereby.
perturbation.explanation: Adding '*what is believed to be*' in Section 12 undermines the 'entire agreement' clause. By qualifying that it is *believed* to be the entire agreement, it suggests that other agreements or understandings could exist outside of the written document, creating ambiguity on what agreements are binding on both parties. This creates an in-text contradiction because section 13 mentions :This Agreement and all rights and oblig ations hereunder shall inure to and be binding upon the parties hereto and thei r respective successors, affiliates, agents, employees and assigns . Neither party may assi gn any of its rights or obligations hereunder without t he prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and all rights and oblig ations hereunder shall inure to and be binding upon the parties hereto and thei r respective successors, affiliates, agents, employees and assigns . Neither party may assi gn any of its rights or obligations hereunder without t he prior written consent of the other party. This Agreement shall be gover ned by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the laws of the State of Califor nia without regard to choice of law principles.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement contains the 'sole and entire agreement'. The changed text modifies this to say that the agreement contains '*what is believed to be*' the sole and entire agreement. This introduces ambiguity by suggesting that other agreements may exist outside the written document. The contradicted text states 'This Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder shall inure to and be binding upon the parties'. If the 'entire agreement' clause is undermined, it creates an in-text contradiction because it's no longer certain which agreements are binding, which directly contradicts the assertion in the 'Contradicted Text' that the current agreement is binding. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities' perturbation definition.
